entry.title: Re: Schedule Daily Delta Accruals - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Ana_Figueira, 

I would recommend to use a Factory calendar, which is set up with daily dates and answering your requirements of execution, for the Delta accruals in the condition contract. When generating the calendar using factory calendar, the system will create every single Settlement date accordingly. 

You can then execute the Settlement via the corresponding tile, and set up a job with a variant created with dynamic date selection for the settlement date if required. 

You also have an option to use the Unplanned Settlement tile available after 2023 version I believe. This tile is pretty much the same as the first one but will create the Settlement date entry in the contract if it doesn't exist. 

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Performance of SAP Screen Personas and RFUI
entry.content:
We’re planning to leverage SAP Screen Personas to modernize our RFUI in a large-scale warehouse environment. Although we’ve successfully implemented Personas on smaller projects, we’re concerned about potential performance impacts at this scale. We’re aware of the significant enhancements delivered in 2023, which is encouraging.

Could you advise on how to estimate any performance degradation based on:

  • The number of Personas scripts and UI modifications

  • The peak number of concurrent RFUI users

Are there benchmarks, sizing guidelines, or tools that would help us predict and mitigate any issues before go-live?

I realize this is a somewhat unusual question, but any clarity or references you can share would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Re: how to compare security roles between users
entry.content:
Hi @KRahilly 

You're right SUIM is a sort of manual comparison.

The best way which i do for my client is

1. Use Table AGR_USERS for direct comparison

Go to SE16N, Enter table: AGR_USERS, Filter for UNAME = 'USER1' and download, Repeat for USER2.
Compare the list in Excel.

2. Run report: RSUSR070 — Display Comparison Between Users

Run RSUSR070, Enter both User IDs, Choose what to compare (roles, profiles, authorizations), Execute.
You’ll get a side-by-side list of roles, with flags showing what is common and what differs.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP, HDI and Datasphere
entry.content: You can also reference the Datasphere HANA DB as the storage for your CAP app. It's not as straightforward as the prior answer, but it allows you to have local data for your Datasphere views and CAP apps.

entry.title: Re: Remove failed requests in Offline Mobile Development...
entry.content:
Hi @akhthaaralibadhusha,

Yes this is possible. For all failed requests during offline synchronization, a new entity will be added in the ErrorArchive entity set. If you execute a delete action on that specific ErrorArchive entity, the request which fails will be removed from the list of pending requests for the next offline synchronization. 

This is documented with sample implementation for our native SDKs, but is also applicable for MDK applications: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/offline/common/handling-failed-requests/reverting-error-state.html#reverting-error-state

entry.title: Re: Enable Custom field in app Create Financial Transacti...
entry.content:
Hi @david-nguyen ,

Adapt UI allows visual placement of the field,however, It does not persist the data unless it's bound to a Business Context
Even if the field appears, it's not stored or transferred to the accounting document.

Check for extensibility support
Go to the app: Custom Fields and Logic (F1481)
Search for Business Contexts like:
Accounting: Journal Entry Item or Accounting: Journal Entry or Cash Journal Entry Item (if available)
If none are linked to Create Financial Transactions, you can’t bind custom fields via this route.

Try workaround

1. Use Journal Entry Extension via BADI (if available)
AC_DOCUMENT_ENHANCE – Enhance Accounting Document
BADI_FINS_ACDOC_HDR_CHG – Change Header Data
BADI_FINS_ACDOC_ITEM_CHG – Change Item Data

2. Switch to Using Journal Entry APIs or the Post General Journal Entries App

If the business process allows, consider using:
App: Post General Journal Entries (F0718)
This app does support extensions via business context: Accounting: Journal Entry Item

Steps:
Go to Custom Fields and Logic
Choose business context: Accounting: Journal Entry Item
Create your field
Enable it in:
-UIs: Post General Journal Entries
-CDS Views: I_JournalEntryItemCube, etc.

I cannot think of any other possibility apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: MRP to create purchase requisition : BADI MD_PURREQ_CHANGE to add G/L Account & Costcenter
entry.content:
Hi ABAP gurus, 

The requirement is change the account assignment category and add G/L Account & Cost center on the new purchase requisition created by triggering MRP ( MD03 ) .

I am able to change the change the Account Assignment Category ( KNTTP ) through the BADI implementation of MD_PURREQ_CHANGE in the Method CHANGE_BEFORE_SAVE_MRP  using  ch_eban-knttp  BUT in the same method the fields G/L Account ( ch_ebkn-sakto )  & Cost center ( ch_ebkn-kostl ) are also there for which i am passing the desired valid values.

However, G/L Account & Cost center are not getting updated. I went through the sap reference note for this SAP Note Number : 1824615 and am using CH_CHANGED = 'X' and  account assignment category (  ch_eban-knttp ) is successfully getting updated. 

BADI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST METHOD PROCESS_ACCOUNT will not get triggered here because ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST  is designed specifically for dialog transactions (ME51N, ME52N) where a user is interacting with the screen. MRP is a background process that does not use the same screen logic, so it doesn't call  ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST BAdI.

Sample code screenshot below :

How can G/L Account & Cost center be added to the new  purchase requisition creation process using triggering of MRP ( MD03 )  using SAP ABAP ?  

Would be grateful to your replies. 

Thanks,

Devendra

entry.title: Re: Transfer historical unbilled revenue to Enteprise pro...
entry.content:
Hello @Nikhil1905 

Thank you for the clarification. 

Unbilled revenue reflected in Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) can only be generated through project-integrated transactions, such as below

  • Time confirmations
  • Procurement or service entry postings
  • Billing plan execution

Manual General Journal Entries, even with project assignments, will not trigger unbilled revenue calculations within EBRR.

For historical unbilled revenue

You can migrate balances at project/WBS level using the Data Migration Cockpit for reporting purposes

However, these migrated figures do not interact with EBRR logic — only integrated project postings can influence revenue recognition

Cloud you let me know if you are working with Fixed Price or Time & Material projects, or specifically Cost-Based POC? (You mentioned Cost-Based POC, just want to confirm the context)

Please refer to SAP Help – Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Projects for Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Review and Adapt Business Roles after a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi,

You asked a good question. One important thing keeps in mind is that all changes are made in D, then transport to T and P tenants.

Before upgrade, you review those upcoming changes from SAP's documentation and make a potential change list. After T tenant is upgrade, you can verify these changes in an upgraded T system. Even you confirm these changes in T tenant, you don't make changes there. Now you have a verified list of changes to be made. Four weeks later, over the upgrade weekend, you make changes in the upgraded D tenant, then move the transports to T and test it in T. When the test is successful, then you move the transport to P tenant.

In short, you don' t make changes in T when it is upgraded.  Changes will be made four weeks later during D/P upgrade weekend. 

Hope this answers your questions.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Splitstorm - A Generic Splitter Configuration Tool
entry.content:
Extracting and Restoring Splitter Container Configurations in SAP GUI with ABAP

When working with complex SAP GUI applications, it's common to use CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER to structure screens. These splitter configurations often evolve over time and differ between users, environments, or sSAPGUI-settings.

This article presents a simple but effective way to extract and later restore the current configuration of SAP splitter containers using a small local ABAP utility that I call SplitStorm.

The core idea is not product-related. It's about treating layout configurations as data that can be stored and restored. While SAP provides APIs to define and modify split containers, it lacks built-in support for introspection and persistence of these layout states.

Problem Statement

There are a few typical situations where this becomes relevant:

  • Users have different SAPGUI settings (theme, fonts or fontsize) which results in different output and hidden information. 

  • Different users have different interest on different areas. One user needs to have a bigger view on the global ALV-Grid, another colleague wants to see more of the detail view.
  • Different screen resolutions result in unused screen areas, e.g. when the ratio is set to 30% for the left object tree, what fits great on FullHD but is to much for higher resolutions or wide screens

Approach

Using recursive traversal and some clever techniques, it’s possible to extract the layout structure and size ratios of a splitter hierarchy. The data is stored in a simple, format that can later be used to reconstruct the exact same configuration.

The main features include:

  • Reading the layout hierarchy of nested CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER instances

  • Capturing the orientation, pane sizes, and nesting relationships

  • Rebuilding the structure with the same layout at a later time

How does it work?

The method ANALYZE_AND_SAVE analyzes the container hierarchy of the given main container. To retrieve the number of rows and columns of a splitter, I simply try to get all containers in a 20x20-Grid. If I do not get a valid container for row 3 and column 4 then I know that there are max 2 rows or max 3 columns.

The height and width for each pane will be read and stored in a simple matrix information

Data will be saved in the Index-Table INDX where data in any format can be stored easily. A better way would be to transform the data to JSON or serialize in a data object and store it in an own table. But for my purposes I think that INDX is okay.

Restoring the data reads the path to the current container and sets height and width. 

It's easy to build the standard layout and the restore the configuration from saved data.

The Code

The actual complete source code is available at github: Splitstorm

Demo report

The Demo report builds a sample configuration using splitter containers without any controls in it.

You can save the current configuration, load or delete an existing layout.

REPORT zt9r_splitstorm_demo01.

SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-sav USER-COMMAND zsave.
SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-lod USER-COMMAND zload.
SELECTION-SCREEN PUSHBUTTON /1(20) TEXT-del USER-COMMAND zdelete.

INITIALIZATION.

  DATA(docker) = NEW cl_gui_docking_container( ratio = 90 side = cl_gui_docking_container=>dock_at_bottom ).

  DATA(sca)   = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = docker rows = 2 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(sca1)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca->get_container( row = 1 column = 1 ) rows = 1 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(sca2)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca->get_container( row = 2 column = 1 ) rows = 2 columns = 1 ).
  DATA(scb1)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca2->get_container( row = 1 column = 1 ) rows = 1 columns = 2 ).
  DATA(scb2)  = NEW cl_gui_splitter_container( parent = sca2->get_container( row = 2 column = 1 ) rows = 2 columns = 1 ).


AT SELECTION-SCREEN.

  DATA(info) = NEW zt9r_splitstorm( sy-cprog ).

  CASE sy-ucomm.
    WHEN 'ZSAVE'.
      info->analyze_and_save( docker ).
    WHEN 'ZLOAD'.
      info->load_and_restore( docker ).
    WHEN 'ZDELETE'.
      info->delete( ).
  ENDCASE.

Main class

CLASS zt9r_splitstorm DEFINITION PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_splitter_info,
             path          TYPE string,
             rows          TYPE i,
             columns       TYPE i,
             row_heights   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY,
             column_widths TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY,
           END OF ty_splitter_info.

    TYPES ty_splitter_info_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_splitter_info WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    METHODS delete.

    METHODS load_and_restore
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS analyze_and_save
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING
        repid TYPE clike.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS save.
    METHODS load.

    CONSTANTS mc_root TYPE string VALUE `ROOT`.

    METHODS analyze_recursive
      IMPORTING
        i_container   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_control
        i_path        TYPE string
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE abap_bool.

    METHODS get_row_count
      IMPORTING
        i_splitter    TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE i.

    METHODS get_column_count
      IMPORTING
        i_splitter    TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE i.

    METHODS restore_recursive
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_control
        i_path      TYPE string.

    METHODS get_splitter_row_height
      IMPORTING
        io_splitter   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_size) TYPE i.

    METHODS get_splitter_col_width
      IMPORTING
        io_splitter   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_splitter_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_size) TYPE i.

    METHODS analyze
      IMPORTING
        i_container   TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container
      RETURNING
        VALUE(result) TYPE ty_splitter_info_tab.

    METHODS restore_sizes
      IMPORTING
        i_container TYPE REF TO cl_gui_container.

    METHODS get_id
      RETURNING
        VALUE(r_id) TYPE char20.

    DATA size_info TYPE ty_splitter_info_tab.
    DATA repid     TYPE c LENGTH 40.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZT9R_SPLITSTORM IMPLEMENTATION.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        TY_SPLITTER_INFO_TAB
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze.
    LOOP AT i_container->children INTO DATA(child).
      analyze_recursive( i_container = child
                         i_path      = mc_root ).
    ENDLOOP.

    result = size_info.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE_AND_SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze_and_save.
    analyze( i_container = i_container ).
    save( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->ANALYZE_RECURSIVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTROL
* | [--->] I_PATH                         TYPE        STRING
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        ABAP_BOOL
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD analyze_recursive.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_splitter) = CAST cl_gui_splitter_container( i_container ).
        result = abap_true.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error.
        result = abap_false.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

    DATA(lv_rows)    = get_row_count( lo_splitter ).
    DATA(lv_columns) = get_column_count( lo_splitter ).

    DATA lt_row_heights TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA lt_col_widths  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH DEFAULT KEY.

    DO lv_rows TIMES.
      APPEND get_splitter_row_height( lo_splitter ) TO lt_row_heights.
    ENDDO.

    DO lv_columns TIMES.
      APPEND get_splitter_col_width( lo_splitter ) TO lt_col_widths.
    ENDDO.

    APPEND VALUE ty_splitter_info( path          = i_path
                                   rows          = lv_rows
                                   columns       = lv_columns
                                   row_heights   = lt_row_heights
                                   column_widths = lt_col_widths )
           TO size_info.

    DATA(index_row) = 0.
    DATA(index_col) = 0.
    DO lv_rows TIMES.
      index_col = 0.
      index_row = index_row + 1.
      DO lv_columns TIMES.
        index_col = index_col + 1.
        DATA(lo_container) = lo_splitter->get_container( row    = index_row
                                                         column = index_col ).
        LOOP AT lo_container->children INTO DATA(lo_child).
          IF NOT analyze_recursive(
                     i_container = lo_child
                     i_path      = |{ i_path }[{ index_row },{ index_col }]| ).
            EXIT. " from do
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDDO.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->CONSTRUCTOR
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] REPID                          TYPE        CLIKE
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD constructor.
    me->repid = repid.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->DELETE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD delete.
    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    DELETE FROM DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND size_info IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration deleted' TYPE 'S'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_COLUMN_COUNT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_SPLITTER                     TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_column_count.
    result = 0.
    DO 20 TIMES.
      DATA(container) = i_splitter->get_container( row    = 1
                                                   column = sy-index ).
      IF container IS INITIAL.
        RETURN.
      ELSE.
        result = result + 1.
      ENDIF.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_ID
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [<-()] R_ID                           TYPE        CHAR20
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_id.
    r_id = |SPLITTER-{ repid }-{ sy-uname }|.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_ROW_COUNT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_SPLITTER                     TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] RESULT                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_row_count.
    result = 0.
    DO 20 TIMES.
      DATA(container) = i_splitter->get_container( row    = sy-index
                                                   column = 1 ).
      IF container IS INITIAL.
        RETURN.
      ELSE.
        result = result + 1.
      ENDIF.

    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_SPLITTER_COL_WIDTH
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IO_SPLITTER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] R_SIZE                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_splitter_col_width.
    io_splitter->get_column_width( EXPORTING
                                     id     = sy-index
                                   IMPORTING
                                     result = r_size  ).
    cl_gui_cfw=>flush( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->GET_SPLITTER_ROW_HEIGHT
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] IO_SPLITTER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_SPLITTER_CONTAINER
* | [<-()] R_SIZE                         TYPE        I
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD get_splitter_row_height.
    io_splitter->get_row_height( EXPORTING
                                   id     = sy-index
                                 IMPORTING
                                   result = r_size  ).
    cl_gui_cfw=>flush( ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->LOAD
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD load.
    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    IMPORT splitter_info TO size_info FROM DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND size_info IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration loaded' TYPE 'S'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Public Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->LOAD_AND_RESTORE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD load_and_restore.
    load( ).
    restore_sizes( i_container = i_container ).
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->RESTORE_RECURSIVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTROL
* | [--->] I_PATH                         TYPE        STRING
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD restore_recursive.
    TRY.
        DATA(splitter) = CAST cl_gui_splitter_container( i_container ) ##NO_TEXT.
      CATCH cx_sy_move_cast_error.
        RETURN.
    ENDTRY.

    READ TABLE size_info INTO DATA(info) WITH KEY path = i_path.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR splitter IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(idx) = 1.

    LOOP AT info-row_heights INTO DATA(lv_r).
      splitter->set_row_height( id = idx height = lv_r ).
      idx = idx + 1.
    ENDLOOP.

    idx = 1.
    LOOP AT info-column_widths INTO DATA(lv_c).
      splitter->set_column_width( id = idx width = lv_c ).
      idx = idx + 1.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Rekursiv weiter
    DATA(index_row) = 0.
    DATA(index_col) = 0.
    DO info-rows TIMES.
      index_col = 0.
      index_row = index_row + 1.
      DO info-columns TIMES.
        index_col = index_col + 1.
        DATA(lo_container) = splitter->get_container( row    = index_row
                                                      column = index_col ).
        DATA(lv_subpath) = |{ i_path }[{ index_row },{ index_col }]|.
        LOOP AT lo_container->children INTO DATA(lo_child).
          restore_recursive( i_container = lo_child
                             i_path      = lv_subpath ).
        ENDLOOP.
      ENDDO.
    ENDDO.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->RESTORE_SIZES
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | [--->] I_CONTAINER                    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CONTAINER
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD restore_sizes.
    IF size_info IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT i_container->children INTO DATA(child).
      restore_recursive( i_container = child
                         i_path      = mc_root ).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.


* <SIGNATURE>---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* | Instance Private Method ZT9R_SPLITSTORM->SAVE
* +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
* +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------</SIGNATURE>
  METHOD save.
    IF size_info IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    DATA(id) = get_id( ).
    EXPORT splitter_info FROM size_info TO DATABASE indx(z1) ID id.
    MESSAGE 'Splitter configuration saved' TYPE 'S'.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: Integrating with the Alerts System API in SAP Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
@Ravisrp

as far as I am aware, there is no standard iFlow or BAdI available for this scenario. You would need to implement a custom solution. An iFlow is not strictly required—it could also be, for example, an SAP CAP service running on SAP BTP. The key requirement is that the resulting data structure matches the expected format.

For more details on the expected result structure, please refer to the SAP Help documentation:
https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SVC/56436b4e8fa84dc8b4408c7795a012c4/71260fe50b7b4114b5fd684471dc6ab5.html

entry.title: Re: Configuring OIDC between SAP IAS and S/4HANA (ABAP) in a RISE AWS Environment.
entry.content:
Thanks for the detailed steps on this!

Just to add some notes based on the experience configuring this:

Troubleshooting:

You can use in the ABAP side the report SEC_TRACE_ANALYZER. Also in IAS you can check in "troubleshooting Logs" and you'll find the details of the OIDC tokens generated and the attributes.

EntraID

If you have your IAS using EntraID as proxy, you'll be redirected to EntraID and same configuration works.

In case you want to configure EntraID directly in SOIDC you can use this issuer:

 https://login.microsoftonline.com/<tenant ID>/v2.0

In Entra you need to configure a new "app registration" but the steps are very simmilar to what you do in IAS

The possibility to use different claims have been introduced in newer versions of S/4HANA 2023 (SP2 or 3 I think) so in older versions integration with Entra was not possible due to the use of a specific attribute "sub" that Entra uses for different purpose than IAS.

STRUST

Make sure you have the corresponding CA certificates loaded in "SSL Client (Anonymous)" as trusted otherwise you'll get an error when loading the issuer in SOIDC

Thanks!

Diego

entry.title: Re: Partial Customer Project Invoice Request
entry.content:
Hi @KCPN ,

I do see couple of ways to do that.

Create Manual Billing Request via “Create Billing Request” App

If you don’t want to use invoice schedule, you can:
Go to Create Billing Request (Fiori app), select the customer project & WBS/item, choose the amount or percentage to bill

Use Billing Plan at Sales Order Level, If you are using Sales Integration.

If your customer project is tied to a Sales Order (SD), you can:
Create a Billing Plan in the sales order, subsequently, define percentage billing or milestones there
Use app - Manage Sales Orders

Partial Billing via API - This is Automated approach

If you’re looking for automation or system-based control:

Use API: A_ProjectBillingRequest or A_CustomerProject

Afterwards,build logic to:

a.Determine partial amounts
b.Trigger partial billing requests programmatically

I cannot think of any other possibiity apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Increase Alternative bank account number field S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello @merle2,

Standard field lengths like the "Alternative Bank Account Number" are system-defined and cannot be extended via key user extensibility for sensitive objects like house bank master data in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

This limitation exists due to compliance and integration consistency across finance processes (e.g., bank statement processing, payment runs).

Options to consider:

  • Double-check the maximum allowed field length in the latest release notes or app documentation
  • Validate with your bank if a shorter account reference is acceptable for electronic statement uploads
  • If the limitation blocks critical processes, raising an SAP support incident is the recommended approach Enhancement requests for field extensions must go through the SAP Influence programs

Reference:

  • SAP Help – Maintain House Bank Accounts (S/4HANA Cloud)

Unfortunately, direct field length extension isn't possible in Public Cloud by design. Let me know if you want help drafting a support ticket.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Customer Project Grouping for reporting purposes
entry.content:
Hi @KCPN ,

Customer projects don’t support full-fledged “Project Hierarchies” like classic PS projects, you can still group them using available fields and tags that can then be used in project-related reporting.

A. Use Custom Fields (e.g., Grouping Tag or Category)
If standard fields are not sufficient, add a custom field (e.g., “Project Group”, “Region”, “Customer Segment”) using:

Within App: Custom Fields and Logic (F1481)

-Business Context: Project: Commercial Project
-Field Type: Code list (for group/category)
-Then enable the field in UI and CDS Views for reporting.

B. Use Work Package Tags / Naming Conventions

While Customer projects don’t support WBS hierarchies, you can:
Use structured naming for project IDs (e.g., REGION-CUST-001) and then add tags or indicators in work package names or descriptions

C. Create Custom CDS Views or Analytical Queries

If you need more advanced grouping/reporting:

Use Custom CDS Views (F1866A) to join:

-Customer project header
-Work packages
-Custom fields (e.g., grouping tags)
-Actual costs (from ACDOCA)

Build a Custom Analytical Query (Fiori app: “Custom Analytical Queries”)
Enable grouping in reporting by your custom dimension (e.g., Project Group)

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP CDC - Dataflow Reader Component: Delta Field for Conditional Updates
entry.content:
Hi Rvanjari,

Can you please open a support ticket so that we can see the exact details of the dataflow? Generally Shahar's suggestion is correct: if you run a dataflow once successfully in "pull all records" mode, subsequent delta runs should pick up all data. If there were no previous successful runs, it will take the last delta time.

Opening a ticket with specific details will let us examine your dataflow and see what went wrong.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: App F0869A , message VL134 when sales B...
entry.content:
Hello @emmanuelm1 

Thanks for your message. The behaviour you’re seeing with error VL134 when using Sales BOMs with mixed EWM and IM stock is expected in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

The system does not allow splitting components of a Sales BOM across different warehouse types (EWM and IM) within the same delivery. This restriction prevents delivery creation when BOM components come from mixed sources.

 Ensure all BOM components are sourced from either EWM or IM locations only.  Alternatively, structure materials as separate sales items rather than within a single BOM

Check SAP documentation below

Outbound Deliveries – SAP Help

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Enabling email notification whenever a PLM ER workflow task is unlocked by admin using SWIA
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

During the PLM ER workflow execution process, if some one executes the workflow task through engineering inbox, then the "processor, Processed at, processed on" fields in ER for that task gets updated.

If some user executing the task and has not completed the task and went on vacation, the task gets locked with him. 

 In such case, only that user who executed the task can forward that to other user. If that user is out of office or in vacation , that task is stuck with him. 

As a result admin will unlock it by using the SWIA tcode in SAP PLM. 

Which means, workflow task status will be moved from "Executed" to "Work item Sent" status. 

Now in this case, when admin unlocks the task for that user , task will go back to all the users in the user group for that workflow task and they can act on it accordingly through "Engineering Inbox" . 

Whenever it is unlocked, it is required for the SAP system to auto generate an email to the Processor/User who is executed the task initially/ the user who is OOO. 

Email message 
Subject : Workflow task (ER# - Task Name) is un-reserved

Body: This is to inform you that the following Engineering Record (ER) Workflow task has been un-reserved:

  1. ER Name: 
  2. ER Type: 
  3. Task Name: 

to do above below config is required in SPRO- PLM WEB UI. (correct me if i am wrong)

Is it possible to add "Send e-mail when the task is unlocked" to the above ?

Where can i define these services in SAP PLM in-order to trigger email notification to process whenever the task is unlocked form the Processor/User who executed it. 

Please advice !

Best, 

Ramu Mannava

entry.title: Re: Sticky bit in Hana client
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, thank you for reporting this problem and this is a known issue. The SGID bit was added accidentally in builds 2.22.29 and 2.23.24 and higher.  This was fixed as issue #340453 in builds 2.24.24 and 2.25.22 and the SGID bit was removed again.

You can also remove the bit yourself manually without consequence: it is not required.

Best regards,
Jeff

Após a execução do job EDOC_BR_DISTDFE_RETRIEVE, observamos que algumas notas fiscais não são processadas corretamente e acabam sendo listadas no relatório de Documentos Pendentes (EDOC_BR_PENDING_DOCUMENT). No entanto, esses documentos não apresentam nenhum erro aparente e, ao reprocessá-los manualmente pela transação EDOC_BR_PENDING_DOC, eles são integrados com sucesso sem a necessidade de qualquer ajuste nos dados.

Alguém já enfrentou esse comportamento? Existe alguma recomendação de análise ou correção preventiva que possa ser aplicada nesse cenário?

Agradeço desde já pela colaboração!

entry.title: SAP Fiori Deployment in 2025: Embedded vs. Work Zone Standard vs. Advanced Edition
entry.content:
As SAP advances its strategy toward a unified and intelligent user experience, organizations are increasingly required to re-evaluate their SAP Fiori deployment models. Traditional architectures, such as the Central Hub, are being phased out in favor of more modern, cloud-based solutions like SAP Build Work Zone—available in both Standard and Advanced editions.

This shift presents a strategic decision point for IT leaders: selecting the most appropriate deployment model that aligns with their existing system landscape, integration needs, and long-term digital goals. Understanding the strengths and trade-offs of Embedded Fiori, Work Zone Standard, and Work Zone Advanced is essential for designing a scalable and user-centric enterprise environment.

This guide compares all three modern deployment approaches so you can make an informed, future-proof decision.

Option 1: Embedded Deployment (Frontend + Backend in S/4HANA)

What it is: Fiori frontend server and Launchpad are embedded directly into the SAP S/4HANA system.

Best for:

  • Greenfield S/4HANA implementations
  • Simpler landscapes with no need to federate apps from multiple sources

Pros:

  • Simplified architecture
  • Lower total cost of ownership
  • Direct and efficient performance
  • No additional BTP subscription needed

Cons:

  • Not ideal for hybrid system landscapes (e.g., ECC + S/4)
  • Downtime impacts entire stack
  • Limited flexibility for UI extensions or content aggregation

Option 2: SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
(Successor to Central Hub deployment)

What it is: A cloud-based launchpad-as-a-service running on SAP BTP. Provides centralized access to apps and content from multiple SAP systems.

Best for:

  • Customers with hybrid environments (e.g., ECC, S/4HANA, CRM)
  • Users needing a unified Launchpad across systems

Pros:

  • Aggregates apps from multiple systems
  • Personalizable Launchpad (cards, themes, roles)
  • Prebuilt integration with SAP apps like Ariba, SuccessFactors, and Concur
  • Low-code extensibility with SAP Build tools
  • Lower entry point than Advanced edition

Cons:

  • Limited support for advanced workflows or custom page layouts
  • Less extensibility for 3rd-party or custom content
  • No native document management or microsite capabilities

Option 3: SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition
(For full digital experience and enterprise portal replacement)

What it is: An enterprise-grade digital workplace platform built on SAP BTP + SAP Launchpad + SAP Work Zone + SAP Build Pages + SAP Knowledge Base. It replaces traditional SAP Enterprise Portal and delivers full-scale intranet functionality.

Best for:

  • Customers replacing SAP Enterprise Portal
  • Organizations needing to integrate SAP and non-SAP apps
  • Use cases requiring business content, collaboration, workflows, and knowledge-sharing

Pros:

  • Advanced page builder for intranet-like UX
  • Deep integration with Microsoft 365, Teams, SharePoint, and 3rd-party systems
  • Role-based content delivery with custom navigation layouts
  • Embedded SAP Task Center, Process Automation, Knowledge Base
  • Ideal for large, multi-LOB organizations

Cons:

  • Higher cost and complexity than Standard edition
  • Requires thoughtful implementation planning
  • Best suited for organizations with mature digital transformation goals

Comparison table:

 

DeploymentOn S/4HANA systemSAP BTPSAP BTP
System Scope1 system (S/4HANA)Multiple SAP systemsSAP + 3rd-party apps
UX FlexibilityBasic LaunchpadLaunchpad + basic cardsFull page editor, custom layouts
CollaborationNoneNonewikis, forums, Teams
Document ManagementNoneNoneAvailable
Task Center / WorkflowBasic InboxFederated InboxEmbedded SAP Task Center
M365 IntegrationNoneNoneAvailable
Use Case FitSimplicity & speedCentral accessFull digital workplace
Ideal ForS/4HANA-only landscapesHybrid SAP appsPortal replacement + collaboration
CostNo additional BTP costMediumHigher

Conclusion

Establishing a unified point of entry for enterprise users is essential to an effective SAP Fiori deployment strategy. Whether an organization operates within a single SAP S/4HANA system or manages a complex, multi-system landscape, selecting the appropriate deployment model ensures enhanced usability, simplified maintenance, and long-term adaptability.

Embedded Fiori is best suited for organizations running a clean, standalone SAP S/4HANA environment. It provides a streamlined, fully self-contained user experience without requiring integration with other SAP or non-SAP systems—making it an efficient and cost-effective option for focused landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Edition is recommended for scenarios involving multiple SAP backend systems, such as ECC, CRM, or BW. It serves as a centralized launchpad, delivering a consistent user experience across the enterprise.

SAP Build Work Zone – Advanced Edition is ideal for companies seeking to replace SAP Enterprise Portal or establish a robust digital workplace. With capabilities for cross-application integration, collaboration, document management, and connectivity to third-party platforms like Microsoft 365, it supports a modern and extensible enterprise UX.

SAP Build Work Zone—available in both Standard and Advanced Editions—represents the evolution of SAP’s UX strategy, replacing legacy frontend models with a cloud-native, scalable platform aligned with digital transformation goals.

Ultimately, the deployment choice should reflect the organization's current IT architecture, user requirements, and future roadmap. Aligning SAP Fiori strategy with these considerations enables a future-ready user experience that empowers employees and streamlines system management.

entry.title: SAP Developer News July 3rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

PODCAST:  https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/07/03/sap-developer-news-july-3rd-2025/ 

Code Connect 2025 event app now available 

  • Event app https://code-connect.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/#/event  

What’s New in SAP Build, Q2 2025 

  • Esmee’s blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q2-2025-release-highlights/ba-p/14142487 
  • SAP Build Discovery Initiative 2025 (sign up to give your feedback): https://go4.events.sap.com/builddiscovery/en_us/home.html 
  • What’s New Webinar: 
  • July 17, 9:00 CEST: https://sap.to/60414diDL 
  • July 17, 17:00 CEST: https://sap.to/60434diDF 
  • Importing BPMN: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/import-bpmn-process 
  • Governance Rules: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/configure-governance-rules 
  • Decisions UI (Studio Home): https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/use-studio-home and Shrini’s news item at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q73gDfWwp-c&list=PL6RpkC85SLQAVBSQXN9522_1jNvPavBgg&index=5&t=89s  

July's developer challenge in SAP Community 

  • SAP Community Escape House for July – SAP Cloud Application Event Hub ⚽️ 🏆 🏟️: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-community-escape-house-for-july-sap-cloud-application-event-hub-%EF%B8%8F-%EF%B8%8F/ba-p/14140520  
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/house  

Marc Bernard: abapGit v1.132.0 is out 

  • Marc Bernard Bluesky post: https://bsky.app/profile/marcf.be/post/3lsgbadkh3k2d 
  • https://github.com/abapGit/abapGit/blob/main/changelog.txt 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Code Connect 2025 event app now available 

1:07 What’s New in SAP Build, Q2 2025 

3:20 July's developer challenge in SAP Community 

3:59 Marc Bernard: abapGit v1.132.0 is out 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for July the 3rd, 2025.

[DJ] Code Connect is just around the corner. Next week, in fact, after a pre-event kickoff with two Code Jams, one on UI5 and one on CAP, we hit the ground running with UI5Con on Tuesday, Recap on Wednesday, and HANATechCon on Thursday. That's a lot of sessions, a lot of topics, a lot of everything. So it's great that the CodeConnect app is now available with everything you need to know, including details on speakers, sponsors, the venue, guides on how to attend on-site and also remotely via streaming, an agenda builder and lots more. The best thing? It's available on desktop and on your phone because it's a native app, web native of course. what else the link to the app no install required is in the description.

[Daniel] the quarterly what's new for SAP build was released this week here is a sampling of what's new first you can help shape the future of SAP build by joining the SAP build discovery initiative 2025, where you can have monthly calls and one-on-one sessions for research interviews and user testing, as well as discussions where you can provide your feedback directly to SAP Build Development. I'll leave a link where you can register for these sessions. Second, you can now import BPMN compliant processes like those you might create in Signavio or NetWeaver or IBM directly into SAP Build. Just open up a project and click the import button. There'll be an option for BPMN processes. BPMN is an ISO standard for specifying business processes graphically. Third, SAP Build is always adding governance features, too worth mentioning. You can now create governance rules to control who has permission to create projects, as well as transport them from dev to QA to production. And as my colleague Shrini mentioned a couple weeks ago you can now provide business users a simplified UI in work zone to update decisions without having to open up the entire project and redeploy it there are also features related to creating UI5 projects CAP projects working with mobile development kit mobile start as well as integrating with SuccessFactors. You can learn about all of the features through two webinars scheduled for later in July. In the description, I will supply links to register for those webinars, to register for the feedback sessions, to Esmee’s blog, as well as all the features that I mentioned today.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. July's Developer Challenge is now available in the SAP community. This month is all about events between SAP Cloud Applications, specifically the BTP service called SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. I highly recommend checking it out, as you will learn how to get started with Event Hub, what's required to set it up, and different important concepts which will help you in your integration scenarios. Similar to previous developer challenges, an SAP Community Batch will be awarded to all participants who complete the challenge. I hope you like it. Ciao!

[Mamikee] ABAP Git version 1.13.0 is officially bringing out powerful updates for ABAP developers. In this release, you can now move repositories to a different SAP package, use ABAP language version support for more object types, view all transports for objects logged across multiple requests, access full support for LXE translations, and also benefit from a number of bug fixes and performance enhancements. These updates were announced by Mark Bernard and you can find the full changelog and his post linked in the description below. Make sure to update your local ABAP Git installation and take advantage of these improvements.

entry.title: ADOBE Forms does not display custom field contents
entry.content:
Hello

I have been facing a situation that would like to share with you and possibly get any help just in case you faced the same situation before.

We had to add a bunch of custom fields in the “Sales Order” and “Sales Order Item” contexts.

Then we implemented two BADIs that populates those custom fields according to a certain set of rules as soon as a sales order is entered into the system.

After the custom fields are populated we need to have them printed in the “Sales Order” output template. In order to achieve that we customized the “Output form template” through Adobe LiveCycle Designer to accommodate such fields. The customized layout was imported back into a new form template and all the required configuration to have it available was performed: output parameter definitions, in S/4, form template assignment and master form template assignment, in CBC.

The sales order gets printed out but all the custom fields shows nothing as if they had no content at all.

After investigating the issue, I realized that outputs with the state “In Preparation” shows the custom fields data. But if the state moves to Completed, the data are no more displayed.

I found some posts asking to extend the data source related to the output template by adding the custom fields. The conclusion is that having or not having the data source extended had no impact on the result.

The custom fields appear in the user interface, the BADIs populate them correctly, the data is persisted into the data base but they do not get printed in the report unless the output state is “In preparation”.

Important to say that the system I am referring to is the S/4HANA Public Cloud, with the Brazilian localization.

Has anybody faced this situation before?

Regards,

Salim

entry.title: How do we use multiple capacities to schedule correctly for forecasting?
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I am trying to generate a capacity plan in SAP to evaluate the maximum required hours by day.

I am successful in creating the forecast, routings, and generating a capacity plan.

The issue is that SAP is pushing the start date for materials to the next day,

Consider an example:

The work center has multiple machines that do the same process so we intend to create one work center with multiple capacities.  However, the work center was configured with a few extra machines to allow for SAP to exceed our maximums (so it wouldn’t push jobs to the next day)

The work center is scheduled based on machine capacity

The routing is configured to allow the system to split the order to take advantage of the extra machines.

Material 4 has a forecast quantity of 3836 loaded on 10/21.

MS01 is run:

Planned order 76641 is generated:

However, the production start date is 10/22/25.

The total required hours for 10/21 for the specific work center across all materials is as follows:

My understanding is that the issue may be because planned orders won’t utilize splitting for capacity.  So how do we allow the system to plan and schedule for equipment with multiple capacities to ensure an accurate maximum demand?

entry.title: Re: Notas Fiscais não processadas após execução do job ED...
entry.content:
Hi @marcos_ed_engelmann ,

The issue you described is a common problem in SAP DRC, often related to:

-Timing or sequencing issues
-Temporary data inconsistencies
-Background job configuration
-Missing or incorrect status flags

Try below solutions and let me know if it worked for you.

1. Review Job Logs and Application Logs
Transaction: SM37
→ Check job log of EDOC_BR_DISTDFE_RETRIEVE for warnings or messages.

Transaction: SLG1
→ Object: EDOC_BR
→ Subobject: DISTDFE or PENDINGDOC
→ This will help identify hidden issues (e.g., incomplete XML, incorrect mapping).

2. Analyze XML and Payloads

When you reprocess manually, the system may bypass certain checks.
Use transaction: EDOC_BR_VIEW_XML to inspect:

-DF-e XML structure
-Schema validation
-Unexpected tags or missing mandatory fields

If there’s no parsing or format issue, it's likely a timing or buffering issue.

3. Check System Time Synchronization

Ensure system time (SAP application server) is synced properly.
Sometimes DF-e documents retrieved too close to the current timestamp are not picked up due to time skew.


4. Increase Wait Time or Retry Logic in Job Variant

Edit the job variant for EDOC_BR_DISTDFE_RETRIEVE:
Add a buffer time (e.g., retrieve documents up to 10 minutes in the past). Add retry attempts if available.

 

I cannot think of any other possibiity apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: HR Trigger functionality is not automatically removin...
entry.content:
Hi @karunakarreddy_vaka ,

I do see possibility that in one the area configuration might be missing. Please verify at your end.

1. Check BRF+ Rule for HR Trigger

Transaction: BRFPLUS
Navigate to the HR Trigger Function used for terminations.
Ensure the decision table includes the “Remove Roles” action for the termination event.

2. Verify HR Trigger Configuration

Transaction: SPRO
→ GRC → Access Control → HR Triggers → Maintain HR Triggers
Check the termination trigger:
Action Type should include Remove Roles

3. Check Request Type Settings

SPRO → GRC → Access Control → Access Request Configuration → Maintain Request Type
Select the request type used for terminations (e.g. TERMINATION)
Ensure:

Remove Access checkbox is enabled
Proper path and stage configuration exists to handle removal

4. Maintain Role Removal Criteria

Transaction: GRACV_REMOVAL_CRITERIA
Ensure all applicable roles are part of the removal criteria so that GRC identifies them for automatic removal.

5. Check Background Job Execution

Job: GRAC_HR_TRIGGER_SYNC (or custom variant)
Transaction: SM37
Ensure the job is scheduled and completed successfully
Review logs to confirm roles are being evaluated

6. Review Plug-ins and Communication

Ensure GRC plug-in components (on both GRC and target systems) are properly installed
At the correct support pack level
Communication is working between systems

I cannot think of any other possibility apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Revolutionizing Legal Workflows: Crafting an AI-Powered RAG Bot with SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Introduction

In 2025, legal departments face increasing demands to process extensive repositories of contracts, case law, and NDAs with accelerated workflows and improved accuracy. A recent McKinsey report estimates that AI-driven automation can reduce legal document review time by up to 60%, driving significant efficiency gains. SAP AI Core, paired with its Generative AI Hub, enables enterprises to build a Legal AI Assistant using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to address queries like “Show me indemnification clauses with Vendor X over the past 3 years” or “Summarize risk in this NDA.” This blog explores the business case, technical implementation, and a sample architecture for deploying such an assistant, leveraging SAP AI Core to deliver context-aware, secure, and scalable legal solutions.

The Business Case for a Legal AI Assistant

Legal teams in enterprises often manage thousands of documents—contracts in SharePoint, versioned PDFs, and legal playbooks—while ensuring compliance with company-specific legal language and risk frameworks. Traditional keyword-based search tools struggle with complex queries requiring semantic understanding, such as extracting specific clauses or summarizing risks. A custom RAG-based AI assistant addresses this by grounding large language model (LLM) responses in enterprise data, ensuring relevance and accuracy.

Use Case Requirements

  • Semantic Search: Enable queries like “Show me indemnification clauses with Vendor X over the past 3 years” by retrieving relevant contract excerpts.
  • Summarization: Summarize risks in NDAs, highlighting liabilities or restrictive clauses based on company-specific risk frameworks.
  • Services and Products Usage: Support queries on legal contracts for services and products usage, such as extracting terms of service or licensing agreement details for compliance reviews.
  • Data Integration: Access contracts and NDAs stored in SharePoint, versioned PDFs, and internal legal playbooks.
  • Compliance: Ensure GDPR and SOC2 compliance through content filtering and PII masking.
  • Scalability: Handle large document volumes and peak query loads during audits or litigation.

Why Custom RAG?

Standard LLMs lack access to proprietary legal data, making them unsuitable for enterprise use cases requiring context-specific responses. RAG combines the generative power of LLMs with a retrieval mechanism that queries a vector database (e.g., SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine) to ground responses in company documents. This ensures the assistant delivers accurate, traceable answers while maintaining version control and data privacy.

Sample Architecture

Below is a system landscape for the Legal AI Assistant, built using SAP AI Core and the Generative AI Hub:

  1. Frontend: A freestyle SAPUI5 application, built with SAP Build Apps, provides a conversational interface embedded in a custom SAP Fiori Launchpad or Microsoft Teams integration for legal staff.
  2. AI Layer: SAP AI Core, provisioned with the “extended” service plan, hosts the LLM (e.g., Anthropic’s Claude) via the Generative AI Hub. The Python SDK orchestrates RAG workflows, including document retrieval and prompt generation.
  3. Data Layer: SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine stores vector embeddings of contracts, NDAs, and legal playbooks. SharePoint APIs and SAP Document Management Service (DMS) provide access to versioned PDFs.
  4. Integration Layer: SAP Integration Suite connects to SharePoint for document ingestion, while SAP Cloud Connector enables secure access to on-premise data sources if needed.
  5. Security Layer: Generative AI Hub’s content filtering and PII masking ensure compliance. SAP BTP’s Identity Authentication Service (IAS) secures user access.

 

Implementation Steps

Drawing from SAP’s AI Core orchestration and grounding tutorial (developers.sap.com), here’s how to build the assistant:

  1. Provision SAP AI Core:

    • In SAP BTP Cockpit, create an AI Core instance with the “extended” service plan to access the Generative AI Hub.

    • Configure entitlements under “Service Assignments” and use the BTP booster for automated setup (SAP Note 3100307).

    • Register a Docker registry (e.g., DockerHub) and a Git repository for workflows, updating .dockerconfigjson with credentials.

  2. Ingest and Vectorize Documents:

    • Use SAP Integration Suite to extract contracts, NDAs, and playbooks from SharePoint via REST APIs or SAP DMS.

    • Preprocess PDFs to extract text, handling versioning with metadata tags (e.g., document ID, creation date).

    • Generate vector embeddings using the Generative AI Hub’s embedding model (e.g., text-embedding-ada-002) and store them in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine.

  3. Set Up RAG Pipeline:

    • Implement a RAG workflow to retrieve relevant documents based on query similarity, using the Vector Engine’s cosine similarity search.

    • Configure the LLM to generate responses grounded in retrieved documents, ensuring traceability to source clauses.

  4. Develop the Assistant Frontend:

    • Use SAP Build Apps to create a freestyle SAPUI5 application, embedding the assistant in a Fiori Launchpad or Microsoft Teams.

    • Implement the UI with sap.m.MessageView for a conversational interface, integrating with AI Core APIs via SAP BTP’s Destination service.

  5. Configure LLM and Prompt Templating:

    • Deploy the Claude model via the Generative AI Hub using the SAP AI Core Python SDK.

    • Define prompt templates to handle specific queries, e.g. 

    • You are a legal assistant for [Company Name]. For queries requesting clauses, retrieve and quote exact text from relevant documents, including terms of service or licensing agreements. For summarization, provide concise risk assessments based on the company’s legal playbook. Ensure GDPR compliance and mask PII.
Context: {retrieved_documents}
Query: {user_input}

    • Enable content filtering and PII masking to comply with GDPR and SOC2.

  6. Deploy and Scale:

  • Deploy the RAG workflow using SAP AI Launchpad, specifying the infer.s resource plan for inference tasks.

  • Enable auto-scaling in AI Core to handle peak query loads during audits, monitoring performance via the AI Launchpad UI.

Code Snippet: RAG Pipeline (Python)

Below is a simplified Python script for the RAG pipeline, adapted from the SAP AI Core tutorial:

from sap_ai_core import AIClient
from hana_cloud import VectorEngine
import requests

# Initialize AI Core client
client = AIClient(
    base_url="https://api.ai.core.sap.com",
    service_key={"client_id": "your_client_id", "client_secret": "your_client_secret"}
)

# Ingest documents from SharePoint
def ingest_documents(sharepoint_url, auth_token):
    response = requests.get(sharepoint_url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"})
    documents = [doc["content"] for doc in response.json()["value"]]
    return documents

# Generate and store embeddings
def create_embeddings(documents):
    vector_engine = VectorEngine(connection_string="your_hana_cloud_connection")
    embeddings = client.embedding_model("text-embedding-ada-002").embed(documents)
    vector_engine.store(embeddings, metadata={"source": "SharePoint", "version": "1.0"})
    return embeddings

# Retrieve and generate response
def handle_query(user_input):
    vector_engine = VectorEngine(connection_string="your_hana_cloud_connection")
    retrieved_docs = vector_engine.query(user_input, top_k=3)
    prompt = f"Context: {retrieved_docs}\nQuery: {user_input}"
    response = client.llm("claude").generate(prompt)
    return response

# Example usage
sharepoint_url = "https://yourcompany.sharepoint.com/sites/legal/_api/web/lists/getbytitle('Contracts')"
documents = ingest_documents(sharepoint_url, "your_auth_token")
create_embeddings(documents)
response = handle_query("Show me indemnification clauses with Vendor X")
print(response)

Challenges and Trade-Offs

Document Ingestion: Extracting text from versioned PDFs in SharePoint requires robust preprocessing to handle formatting and metadata. SAP DMS provides version control if SharePoint APIs are limited.

Latency: RAG pipelines may introduce latency due to vector search and LLM inference. Optimize indexing and leverage GPU acceleration to reduce delays.

Cost Management: The “extended” service plan and HANA Cloud Vector Engine consume CPEA credits. Monitor usage in BTP Cockpit to optimize costs during high-volume periods.

Skill Requirements: Developers need proficiency in Python, SAP Integration Suite, and Kubernetes. SAP’s developer tutorials (developers.sap.com) can bridge this gap.

Comparison with Alternatives

SAP AI Core with RAG offers distinct advantages for legal workflows, particularly in SAP-centric environments. The table below compares it with Azure Cognitive Search, AWS Kendra, and Google Cloud’s Vertex AI Search, focusing on features critical to the Legal AI Assistant use case:

 

FeatureSAP AI Core + RAGAzure Cognitive SearchAWS KendraGoogle Cloud Vertex AI Search

SAP Native IntegrationSeamless integration with SAP DMS, BTP, and FioriRequires custom middleware for SAP systemsRequires custom connectors for SAP dataLimited SAP-specific connectors
LLM Grounding (RAG)Built-in RAG with HANA Cloud Vector EnginePartial (supports embeddings but limited RAG)RAG via Amazon Bedrock integrationRAG with Vertex AI embeddings
Compliance ToolsBuilt-in (IAS, PII masking, content filtering)External setup (Azure Purview for compliance)Manual setup (IAM, custom PII detection)External setup (Google DLP for compliance)
Cost ControlSAP BTP CPEA credits, monitorable via CockpitAzure usage-based pricing, complex meteringAWS monthly fees, higher for enterprise scaleGoogle Cloud usage-based, pay-per-query model
ScalabilityAuto-scaling with Kubernetes, GPU accelerationScalable but requires manual tier adjustmentsScalable with enterprise edition, costlyHighly scalable, optimized for Google Cloud
Document IngestionSharePoint/SAP DMS via Integration SuiteNative SharePoint support, PDF parsingSharePoint via connectors, PDF supportSharePoint via APIs, strong PDF handling
Developer ExperiencePython SDK, AI Launchpad, SAP tutorialsAzure SDKs, extensive documentationAWS SDKs, limited SAP-specific guidanceGoogle SDKs, steep learning curve for SAP

SAP AI Core excels in SAP ecosystems, offering seamless integration and built-in compliance tools. Azure Cognitive Search provides robust document ingestion but requires custom SAP connectors. AWS Kendra supports RAG via Bedrock but lacks native SAP integration. Vertex AI Search offers advanced RAG but demands additional configuration for compliance and SAP data.

Conclusion

SAP AI Core and the Generative AI Hub empower legal teams to build a scalable, compliant Legal AI Assistant. By leveraging RAG, SAP HANA Cloud, and integrations with SharePoint, enterprises can address complex legal queries, including those related to services and products usage, with precision. Challenges like document ingestion and latency can be mitigated through best practices and optimization.

Over the next 3–5 years, deeper integration with IoT and edge computing could enable real-time legal analytics for audits or litigation. Future research might explore combining AI Core with tools like Unity for visualizing legal risk simulations or parametric design tools for optimizing document workflows. To explore further, consult the SAP AI Core tutorials on developers.sap.com.

References: 

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-orchestration-grounding.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/what-is-sap-ai-core

entry.title: How to Identify Production Orders with Staging Performed via Movement Type 311?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

In our S/4HANA system, we are using the Fiori app "Stage Materials for Production" to carry out material staging to the production supply area. For tracking and audit purposes, we would like to find out which production orders have had staging movements executed using movement type 311.

Is there any standard report, Fiori app, or CDS view available that allows us to retrieve this information—specifically, a way to list all production orders that have had a 311 movement posted?

Any insights or suggestions are highly appreciated!

Thanks in advance,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Identify Production Orders with Staging Perfor...
entry.content:
Hi,

To identify which production orders have had staging movements using movement type 311, you can try to use the following options:

  • Material Documents Overview (App ID: F1077)

This app allows you to filter by: Movement Type: 311, Plant, Storage Location, etc.

Once the results are displayed, you can add the columns Order, Goods Movement Type, etc.

  • Display Material Document List (App ID: MB51)

Classic report for material documents. Filter with: Movement Type. Use additional filters like Material, Plant, and Date. Add columns like Order, Reference, or Reservation Number.

From the reservation number, you can trace back to the production order (especially for staging based on reservations).

  • CDS Views for Custom Reporting or Query Browser

If you need to create a custom report or use the query browser, consider using: I_MaterialDocumentItem, I_MaterialDocumentHeader.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Delay data loading of SAP Macros table until button click? - UI5
entry.content:
Hi all - Wanted to know if its possible to delay loading of data from a macros table within a XML fragment? Currently, when you click on a list page record -> open object page -> my macros table in a custom section renders, and loads all table data correctly. All of this data is via CDS entity where I define the metaPath in my XML fragment. Table renders, and data loads in just fine. 

Now, I would like to extend the functionality by adding a button ("Get Data") in the object page custom section, that when clicked, will load the data, and not when user opens the object page. Anybody have any examples of this I can potentially reference? 

Right now, my initial thoughts were using the pre-made controller JS extension file for my object page to try to not bind the data in the onInit() function? Then in my controller file for the XML fragment load the frag, and bind the data upon button click?

 
sap.ui.define(
[
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    'sap/ui/model/Filter',
    'sap/ui/model/FilterOperator',
    'sap/ui/core/Fragment'

], function(MessageToast, Filter, FilterOperator, Fragment) {
    'use strict';
    return {
        onLoadData: async function (oEvent) {
                  ***load fragment -> find macros table ID -> bind elements via metapath to entity?***
        }
    };
});

__PRESENT

__PRESENT__PRESENT

entry.title: BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE performance issues
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are having serious performance issues when using BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE (from a Z program) .

As far we can see, the call to the function is quite standard but it is really quite slow processing Sales documents (we can have hundreds of items per each document). 

Any ideas? Does anybody else have this type of problem?

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Transport Request import ended with RC=8, But able to...
entry.content:
Hello Srinivas V, 

Deleting an already released TR or  ToC could not be a good solution never ever...this is agains CTS best practices.

I mean now the ToC is phisically deleted in the managed system by manual means but in SolMan system in table /TMWFLOW/TRORD_N you have still the entry... see if report /TMWFLOW/TRANSPORT_DELETE could delete this ToC entry.

Next time when the import of a TR, ToC failed try to repair it in the task list for not being considered by ChaRM anymore, or add a new task to the original transport, that is no yet released,  and correct the import error there, then the new create ToC will not failed at import.

Hope this helps,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable now with SAP Identity provisioning
entry.content:
Hi,

If you have turned on SSO to authenticate users, users records are typically automatically created by Enable Now when a new user first attempts to access content.  It is possible to load user records but there is no reason to do that because they will be automatically created as required.  Just make sure that your SSO Server Settings in Manager are set to automatically create user records for new users:

 

 

 

 

 

Regards
Shane

entry.title: PPDS Shelf-life planning and batch derivation SLED conflict
entry.content:
Hello:

We use Batch Derivation on the S/4 Manufacturing side, on our batches. This provides the FG SKU batches the SLED dates based on its Bulk SKU SLED date. However, at Process Order MIGO GR on the FG SKU, the SLED inherited, is over-written by the material master Total Shelf-life data (pls see the attached). To avoid this over-writing, we've to leave the Total Shelf-life material master Plant Data Storage view value blank. This in turn makes the material NOT relevant to Shelf-life planning on the PPDS side (this is standard SAP).

Hence, at this point, we have a conflict between 2 standard SAP functionalities that are causing serious setbacks to the business. Moreover, SAP has also disabled the CIF updates into PPDS for the Shelf-life value of a material master. i.e. we cannot leave the Material master blank for Total Shelf-life & yet have a non-zero value on the PPDS side.

entry.title: Spreadsheet Export in SU53 now displays in German text
entry.content: After upgrading to 2023 FPS 02 (10/2024) , the spreadsheet (export) option was not available in SU53.  Note 3525790 (SU53 Excel export option is missing) was applied to remedy this; however, the (hoover) button text now shows in German 'Tabellenkalkulation' . Does anyone know how to resolve this? Default language is EN.

entry.title: Re: Ledger 4G defined in CoCd ×××× is not active in SAP P...
entry.content:
Hello @ChristopherWan ,

You cannot assign the same Accounting Principle to more than one operational ledger for Asset Accounting within the same company code in S/4HANA Public Cloud.
This restriction exists to avoid conflicts in depreciation areas, asset postings, and financial reporting

For your scenario,

  • Ledger 0L (Leading Ledger) is assigned to the primary Accounting Principle (e.g., IFRS or Local GAAP) and is always active for Asset Accounting.
  • Additional Ledgers like 4G can only be used for reporting purposes or must carry a different Accounting Principle if activated for processes like Asset Accounting.

Full details are available in the SAP resources below

    SAP Help – Ledgers and Accounting Principles

    SAP Learning – Financial Accounting in Public Cloud

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Account object Profit Center differs from that in ass...
entry.content:
Hello @Melle1028 

This message occurs when the Profit Center maintained in the Asset Master Record differs from the Profit Center derived during the migration posting , even if Cost Center and Profit Center setups appear correct.

Recommend solution

  • Review the Asset Master and ensure the Profit Center field matches your intended account assignment logic.
  • Verify your migration template — confirm consistent values between the Asset Master and the fields triggering Profit Center derivation (e.g., Cost Center).
  • Check if any Substitution or Validation Rules (Manage Substitution/Validation Rules app) are influencing Profit Center derivation during postings.
  • Run a simplified test with minimal fields to isolate where the mismatch occurs.

This behaviour is expected and ensures consistent reporting in Asset Accounting. If Profit Centers differ, the system blocks the posting to prevent inconsistencies.

Check the SAP Note below.

  • SAP Note 2887494 – Profit Center Differences in Asset Accounting

With regard

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Account object Profit Center differs from that in ass...
entry.content:
Hi @Melle1028 

This error occurred bcz user activated 'Account Assignment Object' but object assignment not maintained properly,
here below steps you can follow for eliminating this error.

I know bcz I faced this error during data migration.

A. Account assignment
1- Check Account assignment object activation screen by SPRO path

SPRO -SAP REF IMG - Financial accounting new - Asset Accounting - Integration with GL accounting - Additional account assignment objects - Activate Account assignment objects.

2- Then go to t-code ACSET and maintain proper assignment for activated account assignment objects.

B. Please check Field status variant for the Posting key 70.

Pathway : Financial Accounting (New) --> Asset Accounting --> Integration with General Ledger Accounting --> Change the Field status variant of the Asset G/L Accounts

Then pls. go to Change field status for Posting Keys --> Go to Posting Key 70 (Debit Stock) --> Maintain Field Status --> Additional account Assignments , here you make cost center and profit center as an Optional entry.

I cannot think of any other possibility apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Deployment in 2025: Embedded vs. Work Zone Standard vs. Advanced Edition
entry.content: Nice summary and I totally agree for net new FLP usage, but I would add one more nuanced caveat - Embedded FLP is the better interim option for customers who have an extensive S/4HANA FLP footprint already that needs limited integration with additional components. It sucks to open a new window from Fiori Launchpad to access SuccessFactors for example and Central Inbox is a big draw card, but the move to Workzone is quite a change for a previously non-BTP SAP organisation so you need to treat the set-up of BTP as a project in the first place before committing the whole front end to Workzone.

entry.title: Re: Spreadsheet Export in SU53 now displays in German text
entry.content:
Hi @Dezi1 ,

Try below options and see if it works.

Option 1: Check for Missing Language Translations in GUI Status

A. Identify the Program and GUI Status:

-SU53 is part of SAP Basis, usually in program: SAPLSUID_MAINTENANCE
-GUI status may be something like: SAPLSUID_MAINTENANCE > STATUS_SU53

B. Go to SE41 (Menu Painter):

-Enter Program: SAPLSUID_MAINTENANCE
-GUI Status: (look for one used by SU53, or analyze via SE80)

C. Check the Text Elements:

Open the GUI status and check the function code for the spreadsheet (export to Excel) button.
If the button text and tooltip are only maintained in German, maintain them in English manually.


Option 2: Implement Missing Language Translations via SAP Note Correction

A. Recheck the SAP Note 3525790:

-See if manual steps or post-implementation activities are required (especially for text elements or language transport).
-Ensure SNOTE downloaded and implemented the note in English language context as well.

B. If the note does not include EN texts:

-Check if a follow-up note or translation correction note is available (search in SAP ONE Support for “SU53 translation” or “Excel export language”).

Option 3: Use Transaction SE63 to Maintain Translation (if permitted)

Go to transaction SE63 (Translation Tool)
Choose: Menu Entry Translation → GUI Texts → Status / Title
Enter:
-Object type: Status
-Program: SAPLSUID_MAINTENANCE
-Status: (e.g., STATUS_SU53 or relevant)
Maintain English translations for the spreadsheet-related text
Save and test again

I cannot think of any other possibility apart from this. Try to see if it works for you.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Copy from sales order to production order?
entry.content:
Hi @majusko,

Option 1: Use Custom Fields + In-App Extensibility (Key-User Tools)

Step-by-step:

  1. Create a Custom Field:
    • App: “Custom Fields” (F1481)
    • Business Context:
      • For Sales Order: Sales: Sales Document
      • For Production Order: Manufacturing: Production Order Header
    • Field Type: Text (short or long)
    • Enable the field for:
      • Sales Document
      • Planned/Production Order
      • UI and OData services as needed
  2. Enable Field for Use in Both Documents
    • Make sure the custom field is visible in both:
      • Sales Order (via Manage Sales Orders)
      • Production Order (via Change Production Order or Display Production Order)
  3. Create a Custom Logic (BAdI Implementation):
    • App: "Custom Logic" (F1482)
    • Use BAdI: MRP_PRODUCTION_ORDER_READ or similar available BAdIs (depends on scenario)
    • Logic: Read sales order (via reference from MTO link), and populate the custom field or text.

Option 2: Use Text Determination (Sales Order Text → Production Order Text)

This is limited in Public Cloud, but you can:

  • Define text types in Sales Orders
  • If those are part of material master or BOM, use long texts or notes
  • There is no standard automatic text determination from SO to production order in Public Cloud, but you can mimic this using a custom field with long text instead.

Option 3: Use Side-by-Side Extension in BTP

If in-app extensibility isn't sufficient:

  • Use SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) to build a side extension
  • Create a small CAP (Cloud Application Programming) app:
    • Reads sales order
    • Updates custom field or long text in production order via OData APIs

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.
If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Como foi o primeiro SAP Meetup no Rio de Janeiro
entry.content:
O primeiro Meetup da Comunidade SAP RJ foi um sucesso! 
📍 Cafofo Pub, Botafogo – Rio de Janeiro

Na última semana, a energia da comunidade SAP do Rio de Janeiro tomou conta do Cafofo Pub, em Botafogo.

Profissionais de diferentes módulos, experiências e trajetórias se reuniram para trocar ideias, fazer networking e, principalmente, fortalecer os laços fora dos ambientes formais de projeto.

Em um clima descontraído, mas cheio de propósito, falamos sobre carreira, desafios técnicos, tendências como o Clean Core, S/4HANA, e claro, os caminhos para crescer dentro (e fora) do SAP.

Esse foi só o primeiro passo.

A Comunidade SAP RJ está viva, pulsando, e pronta para crescer cada vez mais.

Se você é do Rio e trabalha com SAP, fica de olho nos próximos encontros.

Porque aqui, a gente compartilha conhecimento, histórias… e uma boa cerveja também. 😉
